description: Guard makes a clean setup move on turn 1; patron is killed in place on turn 2, firing A2 (dead character located).
adversarial_note: Exposes a prototype-scope schema limitation: the current ABox delta format has no mechanism to atomically remove a character from located_at. Any delta that sets character_alive=false while the character retains a location entry immediately fires A2 on the same turn. There is no 'clean death' representation at this schema level. The guard setup on turn 1 confirms sequential processing works correctly before the violation turn.

condition_notes.A: ABox never updates after turn 1. Turn 2 oracle sees patron alive at main_hall in the baseline ABox, then applies the delta (alive=false) and detects A2 via silent validation. CVR contribution expected.
condition_notes.B: Turn 1 commits guard→entrance. Turn 2 A2 detected and blocked.
condition_notes.C: Same as B. Per-turn injection reflects guard at entrance on turn 2.

turns.prompt: The guard does a sweep of the room and walks to the front entrance to check the street.
A hooded figure slips something into the patron's drink. The patron shudders and slumps forward, dead, still slumped over the bar.
